creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text: Finally cleaned this up a little and committed it. Sorry it took so long, hopefully updating it makes up for that a little. Also I found that it will only write part of it's default config on the first start, so you have to start it twice, then create the router.db file and then it will startup properly. Which is fine if you know to do that, but there's no way to tell that and no output or log. Sounds like an issue for upstream to address. Anyway, it does work if you config it right. Thanks for the patches!
